summary: Unmerged/closed decomp attempt for sysdolphin baselib hsd_3B34/hsd_3B5C JPEG-related code. The PR replaced large m2c-style bodies and raw byte-buffer field access with more structured C, added an HSDJpegWork overlay struct, moved/retyped several JPEG tables and globals, exposed helper prototypes in hsd_3B34.h, and rewrote IDCT/color-conversion/output routines. The only recorded feedback is the decomp.dev bot report, which showed small .sdata2 improvements but substantial match regressions, including broken .data and hsd_803B5C2C matches; the PR was closed without merge.

key_files.role: main JPEG encoder/decomp rewrite; large table relocation/retyping, helper rewrites, bit-emission macro, hsd_803B51C8 changes
related decoder/JPEG helper rewrites; hsd_803B5C4C, hsd_803B5EA0, fn_803B61B4, fn_803B6820, hsd_803B6BE4 signature/body changes
new HSDJpegWork struct and expanded prototypes for formerly static or loosely typed helpers

what_changed: Added include of hsd_3A94.h and replaced placeholder/string/Gecko_setjmp includes in hsd_3B34.c with <__mem.h>.

Introduced static/global data definitions near the top of hsd_3B34.c, including lbl_804D6398, lbl_804DEB88, lbl_804DEB8C and JPEG quantization/Huffman-style tables such as lbl_80430C40, lbl_80430C80, lbl_80431090, lbl_80431638, lbl_80431678, lbl_80431690, lbl_8043169C, and lbl_804316B4.
Changed several declarations and types, including hsd_804D79BC from u8* to s32 and many function parameters/returns from s32/u32 to int/s32-specific forms.
Added HSDJpegWork in hsd_3B34.h with pad_000[0x118], luma[256], cb[64], cr[64], tmp[64], and pred[3], then used it as an overlay for hsd_804D2648 and hsd_804D2E70 buffers in C code.

Rewrote fn_803B376C from a large m2c-style static helper into a public function taking UNK_T/void* and using two 8-pass loops over s32 data with IDCT-like floating-point constants such as 0.707107, 0.92388, 0.382683, 0.980785, and 0.19509.
Reworked hsd_803B3CD8 to use the HSDJpegWork overlay rather than raw HSD_804D2648_BUF/M2C_FIELD access.
Added HSD_JPEG_EMIT_BITS(code_, size_) macro around bit-buffer output using hsd_804D79AC, hsd_804D79B0, and hsd_803B3344.
Modified hsd_803B4D64 and hsd_803B51C8, including replacing stack-generated JPEG marker/table emission with writes from global/static tables and comment data lbl_803B9670.
Removed or displaced the previous hsd_803B5C2C body in the shown hsd_3B34.c diff; the bot reported hsd_803B5C2C as a broken match.
In hsd_3B5C.c, made helpers such as fn_803B61B4 and fn_803B6820 non-static and gave them prototypes in hsd_3B34.h.
The decomp.dev report recorded matched code at 67.18% (-32 bytes) and matched data at 39.32% (-1104 bytes), with broken matches for hsd_3B34 .data and hsd_803B5C2C.
smart_moves: The HSDJpegWork overlay is a useful tactic for replacing opaque byte-buffer offsets with named luma/cb/cr/tmp/pred regions, making JPEG work-buffer code easier to reason about.

Splitting contiguous JPEG tables into typed arrays such as u8 and u16 tables can make Huffman/quantization logic clearer than a single massive byte blob, though this PR shows it must be done without disturbing expected data layout.
Converting repetitive bit output into HSD_JPEG_EMIT_BITS is a sensible readability move for JPEG bitstream emission and avoids repeating carry/flush logic.
Changing raw M2C_FIELD accesses into indexed s32 arrays in IDCT/color-conversion paths is a good decomp cleanup direction when the underlying work-buffer layout is known.
Adding prototypes for cross-file helpers in hsd_3B34.h made dependencies explicit, but the match impact and symbol visibility needed more validation.
decomp_lessons: For data-heavy HSD/JPEG files, table order, size, linkage, and section placement are match-critical. This PR changed several tables from static/extern blobs into separate definitions and the bot reported hsd_3B34 .data falling from 100.00% to 52.77%.
Do not replace a matched function body or remove a tiny setter without preserving its exact symbol and behavior; hsd_803B5C2C was reported as a broken match, dropping from 100.00% to 0.00%.

Overlay structs are useful, but their size and field offsets must be verified against every raw offset they replace. HSDJpegWork covers offsets around 0x118 plus luma/cb/cr/tmp/pred, so future work should confirm the layout against hsd_804D2648 and hsd_804D2E70 uses.
Retyping globals and prototypes can cascade across files. The hsd_804D79BC change from u8* to s32 and many s32/int/u32 return/argument changes should be treated as matching-sensitive until proven.

Large readability rewrites of m2c output should be staged in smaller PRs, especially for already partially matched functions. This PR combined data-table moves, header changes, helper visibility changes, and algorithm rewrites, making regressions hard to isolate.

Floating constants and casts matter in matching. Rewrites used unsuffixed double constants in many expressions where old code sometimes had f32-style constants; future attempts should test f32 vs f64 literal forms carefully.
Bot reports should be used as gates: small .sdata2 gains (+22 bytes in hsd_3B5C, +13 bytes in hsd_3B34) did not offset large function/data regressions.
naming_conventions: Kept address-based symbol names for unknown HSD functions: hsd_803B3408, hsd_803B3CD8, hsd_803B4D64, hsd_803B51C8, hsd_803B5C2C, hsd_803B5C4C, hsd_803B6BE4.
Used fn_-address names for helper functions without known semantic names: fn_803B376C, fn_803B61B4, fn_803B6820.
Used lbl_ address names for anonymous data and globals: lbl_80430C40, lbl_80431090, lbl_80431638, lbl_804D6398, lbl_803B9670.
Introduced a semantic struct name HSDJpegWork with descriptive field names luma, cb, cr, tmp, and pred while preserving unknown padding as pad_000.
Header prototypes retained address comments such as /* 3B376C */ and /* 3B6BE4 */, matching the project convention for unidentified baselib symbols.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used a typed work-buffer overlay cast, e.g. HSDJpegWork* work = (HSDJpegWork*) &hsd_804D2648 or (HSDJpegWork*) hsd_804D2E70, to replace byte-offset access.
Replaced M2C_FIELD pointer arithmetic with typed s32* loops and indexed arrays for luma/chroma/tmp data.
Factored JPEG bitstream emission into HSD_JPEG_EMIT_BITS(code_, size_), using hsd_804D79AC as bit accumulator state and hsd_804D79B0/hsd_803B3344 for byte output.
Attempted to reconstruct IDCT-style row/column passes with named temporaries and known cosine constants instead of compiler-derived m2c temporaries.
Used global/static JPEG quantization and Huffman tables instead of constructing or referencing large stack/local blobs in emission functions.
Changed helper linkage from static to external for functions shared between hsd_3B34.c and hsd_3B5C.c, with prototypes added in hsd_3B34.h.

review_feedback.summary: GALE01 report showed regressions: matched code 67.18% (-32 bytes), matched data 39.32% (-1104 bytes).
Broken matches: main/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B34 .data lost 522 bytes, 100.00% to 52.77%; hsd_803B5C2C lost 32 bytes, 100.00% to 0.00%.
Improvements only in unmatched .sdata2 items: hsd_3B5C +22 bytes, 63.39% to 83.64%; hsd_3B34 +13 bytes, 66.40% to 76.06%.
Unmatched-function regressions included hsd_803B3CD8, hsd_803B51C8, hsd_803B46D4, hsd_803B4A2C, hsd_803B3408, hsd_803B6BE4, fn_803B376C, fn_803B61B4, fn_803B6820, hsd_803B5EA0, and hsd_803B4D64.
No human review comments or PR body rationale were present in the slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that successfully introduce HSDJpegWork or an equivalent JPEG work-buffer struct in hsd_3B34/hsd_3B5C.
Search for later fixes to hsd_803B5C2C after PR 2479 broke its match.
Look for PRs touching lbl_80430C40, lbl_80430C80, lbl_80431090, or lbl_80431638 to see the accepted table layout.
Compare later hsd_803B51C8 implementations for accepted JPEG marker/Huffman table emission strategy.
Search for review guidance on keeping static data blobs contiguous versus splitting JPEG tables into typed arrays.
